system/blockMeshDict.description: Defines the mesh geometry and resolution. blockMesh reads this file to create the computational grid.

annotations.vertices: The 8 corners of the cavity box. Units are meters. The cavity is 1m x 1m x 0.1m (quasi-2D).
annotations.blocks: hex(...) defines a hexahedral block. (20 20 1) means 20 cells in X, 20 in Y, 1 in Z. simpleGrading (1 1 1) means uniform cell sizes.
annotations.boundary.movingWall: The top wall that moves — this drives the cavity flow. Type 'wall' means it's a solid surface.
annotations.boundary.fixedWalls: The three stationary walls (left, right, bottom). Also type 'wall'.
annotations.boundary.frontAndBack: The front and back faces. Type 'empty' because this is a 2D simulation — no flow in the Z direction.

0/U.description: Velocity field initial and boundary conditions.

annotations.dimensions: [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0] means meters per second (m/s). OpenFOAM uses dimensional checking.
annotations.internalField: Initial velocity everywhere inside the domain. (0 0 0) = fluid starts at rest.
annotations.boundaryField.movingWall: fixedValue (1 0 0) = the lid moves at 1 m/s in the X direction. This is what drives the entire flow.
annotations.boundaryField.fixedWalls: noSlip = zero velocity at solid walls. This is the most common wall boundary condition.
annotations.boundaryField.frontAndBack: empty = no flow in the Z direction (2D simulation).

0/p.description: Pressure field initial and boundary conditions.

annotations.dimensions: [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0] means m²/s² (kinematic pressure, divided by density).
annotations.internalField: Initial pressure is zero everywhere. For incompressible flow, only pressure differences matter.
annotations.boundaryField.movingWall: zeroGradient = no pressure gradient normal to the wall. Standard for walls in incompressible flow.

system/controlDict.description: Controls the simulation: which solver to run, how long, and when to save results.

annotations.application: The solver to run. icoFoam = incompressible, laminar, transient Navier-Stokes.
annotations.startTime: When the simulation starts (in seconds). Usually 0.
annotations.endTime: When the simulation stops. 0.5 seconds is enough for this cavity to reach steady state.
annotations.deltaT: Time step size. 0.005s gives a Courant number < 1 for this mesh and velocity.
annotations.writeInterval: How often to save results. 0.1 means every 0.1 seconds of simulation time.

system/fvSchemes.description: Numerical discretization schemes — how equations are approximated on the mesh.

annotations.ddtSchemes: Time derivative scheme. Euler = first-order implicit. Stable and simple.
annotations.gradSchemes: Gradient calculation. Gauss linear = second-order, cell-to-face interpolation.
annotations.divSchemes: Divergence (convection) schemes. Gauss linear for the velocity convection term.
annotations.laplacianSchemes: Diffusion schemes. Gauss linear corrected = second-order with non-orthogonal correction.

system/fvSolution.description: Linear solver settings — how the matrix equations are solved each time step.

annotations.solvers.p: Pressure solver: PCG (conjugate gradient) with DIC preconditioner. tolerance 1e-06.
annotations.solvers.U: Velocity solver: PBiCG (bi-conjugate gradient) with DILU preconditioner. tolerance 1e-05.
annotations.PISO: Pressure-velocity coupling algorithm. nCorrectors=2 means 2 pressure correction steps per time step.
